intro.headline: RaidGuild Portal
intro.subhead: A coordination layer for a living builder network

sections.headline: What is RaidGuild doing right now?
sections.body: The public home makes current briefs, sessions, projects, posts, and featured activity easier to discover.
sections.consoleText: The Portal is a presentation layer for real activity, not a generic marketing site.

sections.headline: Join, claim, or create your profile
sections.body: Profiles turn identity, skills, roles, links, and contributions into something the community can find.
sections.consoleText: Profiles are not just user accounts. They are how the community finds people.

sections.headline: A lightweight command center
sections.body: Logged-in members can see the current brief, sessions, projects, check-ins, modules, and profile state.
sections.consoleText: Turn passive community awareness into active contribution paths.

sections.headline: Live calls become durable archives
sections.body: A session can become notes, recordings, resources, related projects, comments, and follow-up material.
sections.consoleText: Calendar event -> notes -> recording -> transcript -> summary -> related projects.

sections.headline: Manage the Portal from chat
sections.body: Queen Raida can help create sessions, attach source material, draft CMS copy, and keep community memory current.
sections.consoleText: Queen Raida, create a Portal session for this event, link the Discord event, make it public, and add the summary.

outro.headline: portal.raidguild.org
outro.body: Join the Portal, claim your profile, and help shape RaidGuild's living coordination layer.
